item.title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
item.description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.

item.title: What Makes JD Vance Tick?
item.description: Is deep-seated anxiety what’s driving JD Vance? The Opinion writer Michelle Cottle argues on “The Opinions” that the vice president’s new book, “Communion,” reveals that he remains trapped by childhood scars and a hypocritical obsession with the elite institutions he publicly denounces.

item.title: Will the Real JD Vance Please Stand Up?
item.description: Three Opinion writers share their views on “Communion,” the vice president’s new book.

item.title: We Need to Keep Funding Weird Science
item.description: Science turned a serendipitous finding about lizard venom into one of the most important drugs of the century, but that type of research is getting harder to do.

item.title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
item.description: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.

item.title: My Father’s Death Was the Start of My Life
item.description: It was the worst thing that ever happened to me. It also taught me everything I needed to live.

item.title: Will the Real JD Vance Please Stand Up?
item.description: Three Opinion writers share their views on “Communion,” the vice president’s new book.

item.title: Wait, There Are Two Trump Administrations Right Now?
item.description: The center of American empire is not land but a collection of interlocked state agencies and their business constituencies

item.title: ‘Crony Capitalism on Steroids’: 3 Writers on the World Cup’s First Week
item.description: The history of global soccer, in some sense, is a history of the world’s political problems.

item.title: Art Galleries Are Not OK
item.description: Many gallerists are questioning the fundamentals of their business. It’s as if the art world they know has fallen off its axis.

item.title: There Is No ‘Compromise’ on Abortion Rights
item.description: Readers respond to “The Missing Middle in the Abortion Debate,” an Opinion guest essay by Zaid Jilani.

item.title: Iran Just Taught the World a Dangerous Lesson
item.description: Tehran is the newly empowered gatekeeper of the world’s most important waterway for energy. Other countries will take note.

item.title: Boycott Trump’s Ridiculous July 4 Party
item.description: The president is planning another self-aggrandizing spectacle.

item.title: Trump Put His Own Interests Above All in the Iran Deal
item.description: The Iran deal wasn’t about peace but answering Trump’s personal needs.

item.title: I Keep Telling People We’re Living in This Dystopian Novel
item.description: The 2010 novel “Super Sad True Love Story” foreshadowed our current world. So I asked its author, Gary Shteyngart, how he sees the world today and how we might find pleasure in it.

item.title: I Keep Telling People We’re Living in This Dystopian Novel
item.description: The 2010 novel “Super Sad True Love Story” foreshadowed our current world. So I asked its author, Gary Shteyngart, how he sees the world today and how we might find pleasure in it.

item.title: Why We Delude Ourselves About the Birthrate
item.description: Low fertility rates are a lagging indicator, the final outcome of a string of social shifts.

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Have Really Stepped in It Now
item.description: America and Israel have snatched defeat from victory.

item.title: In Britain, the Far Right Is Moving to the Mainstream
item.description: Its opponents urgently need to demonstrate that they can do more than throw up their hands in horror.

item.title: The Verdict on Biden Is In
item.description: Preliminary judgments on Joe Biden’s one-term presidency, including Jill Biden’s, are all the rage.

item.title: The Democrats Need a Candidate Shake-Up
item.description: The 2024 election showed that the party is simply not large enough to command a majority of the country.

item.title: We Ran the Numbers. Remote Work Is Bad for Us.
item.description: Despite its advantages, work-from-home arrangements have significantly deepened Americans’ isolation and distress.

item.title: The ‘Rivalry’ Between Vance and Rubio
item.description: There’s no “intense rivalry behind the scenes” between JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio, the vice president argues on “Interesting Times.” The Opinion columnist Ross Douthat asks the vice president what happens to his political future if the Iran deal goes poorly.

item.title: JD Vance: ‘I’m Not Fighting With the Pope’
item.description: Did JD Vance privately agree with the pope on the Iran war while publicly fighting with him? Ross Douthat asks the vice president on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: Why the Knicks Really Won
item.description: Teamwork isn’t just useful, the Knicks showed. It’s a higher calling.

item.title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
item.description: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.

item.title: This Is Not a Grand Bargain With Iran. Good.
item.description: The world doesn’t need a nuclear deal with Tehran.

item.title: Trump’s Titanic Insecurities Are Sinking Us
item.description: The president’s titanic insecurities are sinking us.

item.title: The Supreme Court Doesn’t Own the Constitution
item.description: It starts with “We the people” for a reason.

item.title: The Real Trump Resistance Doesn’t Need a Hashtag
item.description: Expand the bounds of whom you are committed to. Reconsider whom you feel responsible for.

item.title: The Real Reason Trump Never Stops Talking About Voter Fraud
item.description: “Voter fraud” is not about fraud. It is about who votes and how.

item.title: Dear A.I. Companies, the Doom Trolling Needs to Stop
item.description: The major A.I. companies keep telling us how dangerous their new models can be, yet they must keep on building them.

item.title: This New Party Wants to Write a New Future for Israel
item.description: A new Israeli-Palestinian political party, A Place For Us All, was born out of joint activism and organizing focused on the daily realities on the ground, not grandiose policies.

item.title: Trump and Iran: Mission Not Accomplished
item.description: Readers react to developments in the war with Iran. Also: Fears about gene editing, a threat to U.S. science; a call to Black voters; reading books.

item.title: The Deck Is Stacked Against Healthy Eating
item.description: We need the will to push back against corporate interests.

item.title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
item.description: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.

item.title: ‘Reddit Posts Are the Median Voter’
item.description: Is the Democrats’ biggest brand problem excessive conscientiousness? The MS Now host Chris Hayes tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein how an institutional, “schoolmarm” personality type alienates swing voters and why an unconventional candidate might be the antidote.

item.title: A Big Moment for American Catholics Is Coming
item.description: When Pope Leo visits Mother Cabrini’s birthplace, the message will not be their shared American nationality but a rebuke of American exceptionalism.

item.title: What We Just Learned About American Power
item.description: How did the world’s richest nation, armed with the most powerful military, arrive at this strategic defeat?

item.title: The Trump U.F.C. Fights Helped Me Understand My Sons
item.description: In my darker moments, I worry I’ve failed them.

item.title: Money and Politics: Tom Steyer and Others Reflect on His Campaign
item.description: Tom Steyer and other readers respond to Michelle Cottle’s essay about his campaign for governor of California. Also: A missed opportunity on the 250th anniversary.

item.title: We Should Expect More From Our Supreme Court
item.description: The Supreme Court is racing to start summer vacation, but the legal journalist Cristian Farias says we have very little to feel relaxed about. In the era of Trump’s relentless power grabs, we should expect the Supreme Court to do more than just “the bare minimum.”

item.title: Good Luck, Kevin Warsh. You’ll Need It.
item.description: Between a surge in interest rates, increasingly restive colleagues and the demands of President Trump, the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh, is in a bind.

item.title: Ossoff: A Cross-Ideological 2028 Dark Horse
item.description: Why has Jon Ossoff emerged as a dark horse for the 2028 presidential race? The MS Now host Chris Hayes and the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein discuss how Ossoff is leveraging a unique “visual grammar” to build an almost mythic, Obama-like political brand.

item.title: The Battle With Anthropic Is the Start of a New Kind of Conflict
item.description: The A.I. wars are here.

item.title: The Democrats’ ‘Upstairs-Downstairs’ Coalition Is at a Breaking Point
item.description: Black and other minority Democrats are a strong force for moderation in a party that is having trouble reining itself in.

item.title: Trump’s Stumble on the Iran War
item.description: Readers respond to news of the U.S.-Iran deal. Also: The affordability crisis; all the wonderful dads; safe to track.

item.title: Iran Found Trump’s Bone Spur
item.description: In a contest of wills, the hard men of Tehran prevailed over the vain man of Washington.

item.title: Bob Dylan and Liza Minnelli Already Turned 80. They Have Thoughts for Trump.
item.description: “I feel the same as I did 50 years ago,” President Trump said. “It’s crazy.”

item.title: What if Both Parties Redistricted to the Max?
item.description: How the scramble for partisan advantage could further distort America’s politics.

item.title: Graham Platner, Jon Ossoff and the New Rules of Political Attention
item.description: Chris Hayes and Ezra Klein discuss how the Democrats are winning the battle for attention, and whether that can translate into winning elections.

item.title: Graham Platner, Jon Ossoff and the New Rules of Political Attention
item.description: Chris Hayes and Ezra Klein discuss how the Democrats are winning the battle for attention, and whether that can translate into winning elections.

item.title: The U.S. Is the Future of Soccer, for Better or Worse
item.description: Thanks to immigrants and the internet, Americans gained entry to a universe beyond our borders.

item.title: Detroit Is the Last City That Knows How to Dance
item.description: Dance floors are flooded with selfies and poseurs. But in one city, it’s still a place to feel free.

item.title: I’m a 53-Year-Old Woman. Stop Telling Me I’m Invisible.
item.description: There’s never been a better time to be a middle-aged woman.

item.title: Does U.F.O. Disclosure Threaten Faith?
item.description: Steven Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ and the God-and-aliens debate.

item.title: Turning to ChatGPT for Help Instead of Your Doctor
item.description: Readers react to a guest essay by a doctor who found ChatGPT useful in dealing with her own medical issues.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein Haunts the White House
item.description: A sleazy situation that Trump can’t wish away.

item.title: Republicans Thought They Owned ‘Values.’ Not Anymore.
item.description: A new politics of morality is rising in the Democratic Party.

item.title: There’s a Better Way to Catch Seafood
item.description: Bottom trawlers kill or maim everything in their path. We should ban — or at least curtail — the incredibly harmful practice.

item.title: California’s Excuses Are Damaging Faith in Government
item.description: Taking a week to count votes is doing it wrong.

item.title: This Could Be the Winning Issue for Democrats
item.description: A.I. is not inevitable.

item.title: ‘A Mighty Form of Communion’: Three Writers on the World Cup’s Strange Magic
item.description: There’s ample cause for celebration and consternation alike.

item.title: ‘All Men Are Created Equal’? Not Everyone Agrees.
item.description: Our founding creed has always had its critics.

item.title: Tom Steyer Spent $558 Million. What a Waste.
item.description: Imagine what Tom Steyer could have done with all the money he wasted on another campaign.

item.title: Why Everyone Wants Jon Ossoff to Run for President
item.description: The Georgia senator is excoriating Trump and his systemic corruption in a way that transcends the Democratic Party’s progressive-moderate divide.

item.title: What A.I. Philanthropists Can Learn From the Gilded Age
item.description: Leave a physical legacy to future generations, not just a record of programs and disbursements.

item.title: The Moms Delivered for Trump. Now He’s Scamming Them.
item.description: The moms are not happy.

item.title: I Need an Escape From Travel
item.description: If I cannot get an upgrade — and I cannot, as I have no status, in any aspect of my life — I would like us to suffer more equally when we travel.

item.title: The 15-Year-Old Keeping War Memories Alive
item.description: Luke Morrison may be the youngest person keeping alive an age-old tradition: to process war through the memories and mementos of those who experienced it.
